stub: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: He frequently speaks out and votes against his party leadership.
substantive: He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, he regularly explains how his policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
text: He is a Conservative MP who comes from a white working class background. He is from your constituency. He frequently speaks out and votes against his party leadership. He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, he regularly explains how his policies matter to people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “If I lose an election doing what I think is right, then so be it.”
